final_answer: COVID-19 symptoms often show up 2 to 14 days after contact with the virus. Common symptoms include congestion, runny nose, or sore throat, dry cough, shortness of breath, loss of taste or smell, extreme tiredness (fatigue), digestive symptoms like upset stomach, vomiting, or diarrhea, pain such as headaches and body or muscle aches, and fever or chills [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002].
